Please feel free to resign if this is not what you would like.  The game is unranked and you should not suffer any penalty
C[Focogogo: Thanks, I understand. Im at work as well.
C[Focogogo: That being said, you'll have to forgive my poor playing sometimes. New player + distractions, you know.
C[quiettimes: No judgment here.  Same lane.
C[quiettimes: I'm sorry for the delay, I guess the submit button didn't register
C[quiettimes: I apologize.  When you left D4 adn D6 separated, I took advantage.
C[quiettimes: This is the same thing.  When you separate your stones, you do not allow them to support each other.  Just to show.. Feel free to request take back
C[Nastur: nah, that's just my way of doin it))
C[quiettimes: Fabulous.  I appreciate your style.
C[Nastur: ok, now this one sure is awkward)))
C[quiettimes: The loss of two stones for the corner is a good trade.  I think.
C[Nastur: we'll see about that corner))
C[Nastur: i mean snap back or no snap back, i'm done there:(
C[quiettimes: True. I was just happy to recognize the opportunity. My brain shorted out after that.
C[quiettimes: I think that is the first time I ever recognized one in the wild
C[Nastur: oh boy, not the double-atariing again
C[quiettimes: I do apologize.  It just goes back to the earlier comment to encourage solid play when in close quarters.
C[Nastur: yeah-yeah, it's obviously not your fault but mine.
quiettimes: I do not mean to come and play own the board. It is not fun for either of us.
C[Nastur: i always end up opening myself up to an attack like that
C[quiettimes: I used to do the same thing!  More plays, more fun.
Nastur: i guess it'll pass with practice
C[Nastur: at least I had those 2 stones am i right))
quiettimes: Yes, those ones were definately yours
C[Nastur: yeah, gotta have priorities in life
C[quiettimes: True that.  Another Go meme.
C[quiettimes: Just sayin...When I am judging these spaces, I ask two questions.  1. Can I make two eyes  2.  Will my opponent let me. Can I prioritize another area for them?
C[Nastur: well i don't really have other areas anymore and there's always a chance of my opponent having a sudden stroke or something
quiettimes: Just casting out some thoughts.  I do not mean to do anything but add to the game.
C[Nastur: it's nice to get some critisizm
C[quiettimes: Never criticism.  Just sharing mistakes I made in the past.

quiettimes: thank you for playing!  much appreciated.
quiettimes: I do hope we get to play again.

C[quiettimes: Hello, thank you for playing.
C[quiettimes: Your skills are admirable for a 26k, and mine poor for a 19k
TARAHUMARA: is the handicap, it helps lots

quiettimes: I will pause for the possibility of a disconnect
quiettimes: Tarahumara, please let me know when you return
quiettimes: Well, I think this game is a loss to me.  Judicata, you think there is any hope of a recovery?
quiettimes: My thought is that I have lost 3 corners and the obscene moyo on the right side is pretty much inpenetrable.
judicata: can you see my comments?
quiettimes: Well this game is lost and I am not totally convinced it is by a 6 stone handi alone.
quiettimes: Tired of losing too, quite honestly.
C[quiettimes: np.  I Have a bad connection and need grace at times too
TARAHUMARA: lol it happens lots to me
C[judicata: G8 is really important for both sides.
C[judicata: i hope you guys can't see that comment.

quiettimes: okie.  Thats my sign that I'm a fool
quiettimes: embarassing Judicata.  And you want to remind me?
judicata: can I show you something?
quiettimes: I think so.  We can play it out.
C[AHHButcher: wait is it giving you negative komi?
AHHButcher: (I didn't know that ever happened)
C[quiettimes: Have you been studying openings?
quiettimes: Your response is part of a standard opening.
AHHButcher: I have not studied openings.
C[AHHButcher: It just seemed the smart thing to do.
AHHButcher: now you can make two eyes easily
C[quiettimes: I can however who made out with the better exchange?
C[quiettimes: oh!  I like that move!
C[AHHButcher: I'm a try to build a ladder!
C[quiettimes: That is a good concept, and advanced concept. We really need to set aside a couple hours and talk about these.

C[quiettimes: I had heard about your vacation and excited to see you back. I have not heard about Unearthed however I will look into it today.
quiettimes: Welcome back and Happy Resurrection day!
AHHButcher: Opps I didn't realize how much time I had left.
C[ethan.vizitei: Thank you for the game
C[Tom Newman: Great to hear from you - thanks for the challenge!
C[quiettimes: Going for a fast start.
C[quiettimes: Hello Tom. Took a break as I'm prone to do. Thank you for accepting.  Hope all is well with you.
C[Tom Newman: What part of Canada are you in?  If you are like us, the days are getting shorter so less outside time and more for go.
quiettimes: Well I am still a Canadian citizen however I have been living in Kentucky for the last 12 years.
C[Tom Newman: Does that make you a southern Canadian?
C[Tom Newman: all is well here. You are probably in luck.  I've been doing OK the last few months.  Time for me to go on a losing streak!
C[quiettimes: Dark tidings Tom. I want your best game.
quiettimes: Well q17 might have been a mistake but hopefully recoverable.  My thought is that he close up the side and I will extend out a bit.
C[quiettimes: Trying to stay relevant while being aggressively expansive.
C[quiettimes: More for expansion vs fight for that little spot on the top right corner.

Tom Newman: Move 16 seems like a big decision.  If I respond to C14 with something like G16, I feel like Black has 1/2 the board (left and bottom).  If I ignore C14 and respond with R16, then I risk being invaded  on the top.
Tom Newman: I think I should fix the top first, but worry it's giving too much territory to Black.
C[quiettimes: I'm struggling with this move and connecting to make a line of three. Its not aggressive enough and I am concerned Tom will take the lead.
C[quiettimes: By the way, I am using Malkovich log.
C[Tom Newman: excellent.  I'm trying to as well.
quiettimes: Struggle with the decision on this movie. Was really thinking of going c3 but I decided to keep the initiative instigate threats over on the right side middle
Tom Newman: Comment above should have pointed to R6, not R16.
Tom Newman: Black's move 21 was what I was worried about - giving up too much on the bottom right.
Tom Newman: Black may have jumped too far with a three space jump.  I'll try to exploit this.
C[quiettimes: I chose R 4 vs q3 simply because our floor was closer to q8.
C[quiettimes: The problem is that I really want to play the 3-3 @ c3.  I just know that when they're opening has run its course going to go there next.
C[Tom Newman: During the 3-3 invasion, black played N2 instead of the expected N3.  Will try to exploit this.
C[Tom Newman: If only I knew the best way to exploit it.  Guessing here is probably a bad thing to do.  Note to self - figure out what to do before playing a guess and hope move.
C[quiettimes: I like r6. Was bummed out slogging the this corner. Trying to stay fast. IMHO, this little move makes up for it.

Tom Newman: OK the 3-3 invaision worked out ok.  I'll try to do the same agains the Lower Left corner next, but worry that doing that will then give Black too much of a large framework in the variation above.
Tom Newman: So before I try the 3-3 I'll try to reduce a little of that first, then invade.
Tom Newman: I think, threating to cut by playing at N6 will allow me to make a couple of moves that I can later build from.
C[Tom Newman: Again I'm confused by Black's response to the 3-3 invasion.  Was expecting B7 instead of C2.
Tom Newman: correction C7 instead of C2
Tom Newman: So I think I'll try to get out at C7.  Instead of just being limited to a small area in the corner, I may have a chance to do some damage now.
C[Tom Newman: Perhaps Black thought to play C2 because he has a stone at D10?
C[quiettimes: Well to sum up so far, game resembles little of what I had envisioned.  I have a good spot b/t C and K.  Will likely break ou at F15
Tom Newman: I think N12 is the best response to Q12.will be interesting to review later which is the better choice - inside or outside.
Tom Newman: I men o12 not n12
C[quiettimes: Well the upper left is not a good trade but I walked away with something....  Going to have to reduce it at some point at J16
quiettimes: As for skipping out, I think I can survive and put Q10 in a bad place.  That would make me very happy.

C[quiettimes: This board position is fascinating (to me)  I almost missed Tom making a connection to the top right corner.  Doesn't improve my situation but cool to see. Even if I threat and block the connection he will still live happily ever after in the right corner.
C[quiettimes: Regrettably last few moves were on my phone and texting doesnt work as well so no comments.  Suffice it to say, Tom has done what I figured he would, just thankful I caught it.
quiettimes: I always get a little grumpy when I'm out maneuvered.
Tom Newman: I think R15 will give me a little more terrority and a better shape.
C[Tom Newman: It looks like black has done a nice job of staying connected on the right.  I need to seal off the top right, then work on getting a better framework in the center
C[quiettimes: Odd exchange. White for the better.
C[quiettimes: Granted,  the 3 step leaves a hole @ m16 however if I connected with the two stones connected he would have bowled over me and made an incursion on my right side after.
quiettimes: F14 is a solid play.  I know there is a hole to exploited here when he runs into the center
C[quiettimes: G10 is to make a presence in The middle left side.
Tom Newman: J11 seems like a safe move and helps the white framework, but leaves black more area.  I think h10 is more aggressive but a higher risk.
C[quiettimes: D9 to turn the corner and firm up the EFG lanes. Still worried about what's going on in the top right corner.
Tom Newman: I think I can ignore D9 for now and try to make a bigger move at P8.  If Not, then P8 would be a large move for Black.
C[Tom Newman: The largest area appears to be in the center around G8.  It has the largest potential for black, so I'll try to push into this area and reduce the potential.
C[quiettimes: Well.  Failed on using malcovich.  Tuff to keep up with.
quiettimes: I really need to work on counting.
quiettimes: I am definately behind.  Thought I was doing a better run than this.
C[quiettimes: Going to close off groups and force sente as much as possible. We are pretty much at the end game.
Tom Newman: A - would be large for black to reduce whites area.
Tom Newman: B - this area is large for black
Tom Newman: C - this area is still open. White needs to make a move before black can seal it off.
Tom Newman: D - another large area for black, but more solid than B
Tom Newman: Will try to make a move to secure the area around A, maintain sente and then invade reduce around B
quiettimes: Ignore the review.  My bad attempt at using analyze.

quiettimes: I tell you it's really frustrating trying to play ladder.  Four or so games is more than I am willing to keep up with in Corr and then you time out and all games are bounced out of the ladder.  Very frustrating.  I guess I must fall back to live only.  Speaking of which we should line up one some day.  A live game.
Tom Newman: I would be interested in that.  This weekend may be good for me.  My wife is out of town and if an RFP I'm waiting for doesn't drop I'll have every evening free.
quiettimes: Well, ballsy invasion attempt.  Dont know which direction he will run.  Close off one and then the other.  This way, I will create a tiger jaw in the DE14 area.

Tom Newman: So I played E12 without a very good plan of how to connect him.  I'm going to try and use it to push in and hopefully keep black on the defense while I nibble at the edges to reduce his terrority.
C[Tom Newman: and, for this game don't worry about the time.  I'm more than happy to pause it to make sure time doesn't figure into the outcome.
Tom Newman: Looks like the area around B and C have the largest impact.
C[Tom Newman: Lose sent with A16 but secures the corner.
C[quiettimes: Well Tom, you have pretty much destroyed everything I have worked for.
quiettimes: I really must flip to live
C[Tom Newman: If you are interested in a live game this weekend let me know.
Tom Newman: trying to get some reduction before connecting..
C[quiettimes: Sure. This game is ugly.
C[Tom Newman: that was dumb....  I keep forgetting this group is connected.
C[quiettimes: It was my last hurrah.  I have nothing left
C[quiettimes: Regrettably, my malkovich was brief.  Started strong and then waned dramatically.
Tom Newman: I look forward to reading it.
C[quiettimes: It will disappoint.  Don't know what happened this time around.
Tom Newman: Interesting to see the thought process, even just a glimpse of it.
C[quiettimes: Any thoughts on a computer beating a pro?
C[Tom Newman: Always thought it would happen, just wasn't expecting it so soon.  Pretty impressive.  Not sure they will be Lee though.
Tom Newman: Will be fun to watch. Did you see the review on the AGA channel?
quiettimes: I think, reading between the lines that the live player didn't take the comp seriously in the first game, cautious in the second and mental breakdown in the last two
quiettimes: Reading between the lines from an interview with him
Tom Newman: Its a pretty good review.
quiettimes: Other pro's I read were encouraged by how ""human"" the comp played
Tom Newman: I wonder if they have enough modern 9P vs. 9P games to train on to get as strong as Lee Sedol.
quiettimes: Thank you.  I will watch tomorrow.  I have to run tom.  I may avoid these pbm games for a while though I do hope you know I very much enjoy our games.
Tom Newman: I enjoy them as well.  Looking forward to playing again whenver you are free.

C[Debroos: I beg your pardon - just noticed Canadian flag..
Debroos: Where does the chat line come from with the live game option?  At my end it appears as a window distinct from the Correspondence chat slot.

C[quiettimes: Was thinking of playing conservatively and splitting the corner in some way,  however giving him an opportunity to play R4 would give him a ridiculously good exchange so decided to treat corner in a traditional Joseph kind of way.

C[quiettimes: If I don't reinforce this ?cut?  then he will cut me and the whole move is pointless. I expect Debroos t hit Q3 for the extension.  And the reality is that I may have separated the two groups of black stones, however Black got the better deal.. Me thinks
C[Debroos: How's the weather in your neck of the woods? We're getting almost perpetual wind and rain
C[quiettimes: Last couple days - 20 to - 30'C.   Ridiculous.  I moved for this (amongst ither) reasons.
Debroos: Tha is phenomenal.  We've seen some footage on tv - unimaginable
quiettimes: I went high on 4 with hopes of either pincing the lower star point or building into corner and setting up a 3-3 invasion.
C[quiettimes: And then a thunder shower  few days later, followed by light flurries.
quiettimes: I tell you no lie.  There was one year that it was a snow fall, with a lightning storm.  No lie.  Rained later.
quiettimes: Lightning during a light flurry does bring a second coming to mind.
C[Debroos: I'd opt for a sunny day with a light breeze if it was me.  People might feel good and be more receptive.

C[quiettimes: I took R8 with this thought.  It's early in the game and I need to play for bigger game vs local fight.  I took 3rd line to block a two point extension by black from R6-R8 and have a good sized territory on the right side.

C[quiettimes: I ran through a lot of variations on the R group, trying to convince myself it is worth saving.  I don't know that it is at this  point.  Even if I save it, it's going to be a burden as it provides no real territory.  Rather, I ended up saying to myself, what happens if I ignore that group all together.  Whats left?  Decided to isolate black Q group a little more and make some territory.
C[quiettimes: I am officially disgusted in my reading of this board.
Debroos: You seem to be doing ok..
Debroos: My reading skills in all my games leave much to be desired.  I've just started watching some of Nick Sibicky's games on youtube to see if I can improve mine.

C[Debroos: Further to the above - my last move a case in point where I was clearly out to lunch at the time - meantime pursuing the flow of events after my cut at L8, overlooking the imminent death of the two fellow conspirators.
C[Debroos: woops.  I missed that altogether..
C[quiettimes: I played a brutal correspondence gone live game the other day.  I played so bad I damn near gave up the game entirely.
Debroos: I've considered quitting this one a few times - it's looking fairly dire but I'll keep going for a while longer
C[Debroos: I think I've had it .... very instructive game though, so if you fancy another do let me know or fire off a challenge.
C[quiettimes: Thank you.  Your avatar, D2 or maybe SC?
quiettimes: yes, almost. I was saying a few colorful words right around then.
quiettimes: Good game.  Maybe a live one some time.
C[quiettimes: thank you for the game. Don't see where I could recover.
